member.name: Ian Roome

addresses: The Castle Centre, 25 Castle Street, Barnstaple, North Devon, EX31 1DR

member.synopsis: Ian Roome is the Liberal Democrat MP for North Devon , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.

writtenQuestions.heading: Mobile Broadband: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps her Department is taking to compare the quality of consumer mobile phone signal in specific geographic areas against the quality of service assurances published by mobile net
writtenQuestions.answerText: The regulator, Ofcom, is responsible for reporting on mobile network coverage across the UK. Ofcom’s reporting in their Connected Nations reports and the coverage maps available in the ‘Map Your Mobile’ tool, is based primarily on coverage predictions fro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Solar Power: Nature Conservation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his department is taking to ensure that the development of solar farms supports the protection and enhancement of wildlife and natural habitats.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All solar projects are subject to a rigorous planning process, in which the impacts on wildlife and habitats are considered. Biodiversity net gain is also a legal requirement under the Environment Act 2021 and associated regulations. This means that to ac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Arctic: Marines

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is continuing to transform the UK Commando Force into a more agile, lethal and technology-enabled warfighting formation, optimised for operations in the Euro-Atlantic and High North alongside NATO Allies. The £100 million announced for new ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Countryside Stewardship Scheme and Sustainable Farming Incentive
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to introduce an offline application process for the Sustainable Farming Incentive and the Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier Scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Rural Payments Agency recognises the importance of ensuring that farmers and land managers can access environmental land management schemes. Applications for the Sustainable Farming Incentive and Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier are made through th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Countryside Stewardship Scheme and Sustainable Farming Incentive

writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra understands the importance of Commons and recognises the importance of bringing them under these schemes. For technical reasons, commons groups will not be able to apply for SFI on common land when the application window opens later this month or ap...

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what criteria she will use to designate social media platforms included in a ban for those under 16 years of age; and whether she will regularly review the criteria for inclusion.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Keeping children safe online is a priority for this government. That is why the Secretary of State announced a ban on social media platforms offering their services to under-16s. We aim to use the same model as Australia. This means capturing user to user...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment they have made of the adequacy of guidance on the treatment of established mould in homes; and whether he has had discussions with industry bodies in the housing,

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Houses: Planning
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to help ensure that the National Planning Policy Framework minimises the number of community pubs lost to closure.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to question UIN 120463 on 23 March 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Shipping: Territorial Waters
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to verify the claimed registered nationalities of ships sailing in UK territorial waters.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government continuously monitors UK waters to ensure the safety of mariners, protect the marine environment and uphold the UK's national security.Where steps are required to verify the flag status of a vessel, this is done on a case-by-case basis in c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: School Meals: Nutrition
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to support schools to provide nutritious school meals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are committed to raising the healthiest generation ever and we are consulting on proposed updates to the School Food Standards, closing on 12 June. The changes we propose to make are related to increasing fibre, reducing sugar and further restricting f...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: School Meals: Nutrition
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department plans to update the Requirements for School Food Regulations 2014 in line with current dietary advice.

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Health: Local Government Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether the size of the Public Health Grant provided to local authorities is proportional to the population of each local authority.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Public Health Grant allocations have been confirmed for the next three years to enable local authorities to plan and make the best use of the funding available. The majority of funding which makes up the Public Health Grant will continue to be distributed...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HM Coastguard: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has prepared contingency plans against any significant reduction in the number of personnel available to support the Coastguard Rescue Service.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Public safety remains our priority. Contingency plans are in place to enable the continuation of maritime and coastal search and rescue.Alongside the Coastguard Rescue Service, HM Coastguard will continue to draw upon the full UK Search and Rescue system,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Merlin Helicopters: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps his Department is taking to review safety checks carried out on Royal Navy Merlin Mk2 and Mk4 helicopters.
writtenQuestions.answerText: A full investigation is being conducted into the cause of the crash involving a Royal Navy Merlin helicopter on 3 June 2026. All military flying operates under robust Military Aviation Authority regulations, with Royal Navy aircraft subject to rigorous an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Dentistry: Apprentices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what plans the Department have to provide additional funding to support the delivery of the dental hygienist apprenticeship where the cost of clinical training exceeds the apprenticeship funding band.
writtenQuestions.answerText: To ensure the overall affordability of the programme, the maximum government contribution to the cost of any apprenticeship, including the level 6 dental hygienist standard, is £27,000. Employers are able to negotiate a price for their apprentice’s traini...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions
